$KMnO_4$ में Mn की 'ऑक्सीकरण संख्या' क्या है?
e: $+1 + x + 4(-2) = 0 \Rightarrow x = +7$. / इसकी गणना $+1 + x - 8 = 0$ के रूप में की जाती है।

q:
Which of the following is NOT a colligative property?
निम्नलिखित में से कौन सा अणुसंख्य गुणधर्म नहीं है?
e: Surface tension is a property of the liquid itself, not dependent on particle count. / पृष्ठ तनाव तरल का अपना गुण है, कणों की संख्या पर निर्भर नहीं।

q:
What is a 'Reducing Agent'?
'अपचायक' (Reducing Agent) क्या है?
o: Donates electrons | इलेक्ट्रॉन दान करता है
e: A reducing agent gets oxidized itself while reducing others. / अपचायक दूसरों का अपचयन करते हुए स्वयं ऑक्सीकृत हो जाता है।

q:
Why is 'Salt' spread on icy roads in cold countries?
ठंडे देशों में बर्फीली सड़कों पर 'नमक' क्यों छिड़का जाता है?
o: To lower the freezing point of water | पानी के हिमांक को कम करने के लिए
e: Salt causes 'Depression in Freezing Point', melting the ice at lower temps. / नमक 'हिमांक में अवनमन' पैदा करता है, जिससे बर्फ पिघल जाती है।

q:
What is the 'Oxidation State' of Carbon in $CO_2$?
$CO_2$ में कार्बन की 'ऑक्सीकरण अवस्था' क्या है?

Which instrument is used to measure 'Osmotic Pressure'?
'परासरण दाब' मापने के लिए किस उपकरण का उपयोग किया जाता है?
o: Berkeley and Hartley's apparatus | बर्कले और हार्टले का उपकरण
e: It is a standard method for determining osmotic pressure of solutions. / यह विलयनों के परासरण दाब को निर्धारित करने की एक मानक विधि है।

q:
A reaction in which both oxidation and reduction occur simultaneously is called:
वह अभिक्रिया जिसमें ऑक्सीकरण और अपचयन दोनों एक साथ होते हैं, कहलाती है:
e: Reduction + Oxidation = Redox. / अपचयन + ऑक्सीकरण = रेडॉक्स।

q:
What is the 'Van't Hoff Factor' (i) for $NaCl$ in water (assuming 100% dissociation)?
पानी में $NaCl$ के लिए 'वैन हॉफ फैक्टर' (i) क्या है (100% वियोजन मानते हुए)?
e: $NaCl \rightarrow Na^+ + Cl^-$ (total 2 particles). / $NaCl$ दो कणों में टूट जाता है।

q:
What is the oxidation number of Hydrogen in 'Metal Hydrides' like $NaH$?
$NaH$ जैसे 'धातु हाइड्राइड' में हाइड्रोजन की ऑक्सीकरण संख्या क्या है?
e: Hydrogen usually has +1, but in metal hydrides, it is -1. / हाइड्रोजन आमतौर पर +1 होता है, लेकिन धातु हाइड्राइड में यह -1 होता है।

q:
The 'Elevation in Boiling Point' is represented by which formula?
'क्वथनांक में उन्नयन' को किस सूत्र द्वारा दर्शाया जाता है?
o: $\Delta T_b = K_b \cdot m$ | $\Delta T_b = K_b \cdot m$
e: Here m is molality and $K_b$ is ebullioscopic constant. / यहाँ m मोललता है और $K_b$ मोलल उन्नयन स्थिरांक है।

q:
In the reaction $Zn + Cu^{2+} ightarrow Zn^{2+} + Cu$, which species is 'Reduced'?
अभिक्रिया $Zn + Cu^{2+} ightarrow Zn^{2+} + Cu$ में, किस प्रजाति का 'अपचयन' होता है?
e: Cu2+ gains two electrons to become Cu metal. / Cu2+ दो इलेक्ट्रॉन ग्रहण करके Cu धातु बन जाता है।

q:
Which colligative property is used to find the 'Molar Mass' of polymers and proteins?
पॉलिमर और प्रोटीन का 'मोलर द्रव्यमान' ज्ञात करने के लिए किस अणुसंख्य गुणधर्म का उपयोग किया जाता है?
e: It provides measurable values even at very low concentrations. / यह बहुत कम सांद्रता पर भी मापने योग्य मान प्रदान करता है।

q:
What is the 'Oxidation Number' of Sulfur in $H_2SO_4$?
$H_2SO_4$ में सल्फर की 'ऑक्सीकरण संख्या' क्या है?

What is the 'Freezing Point' of an ideal solution compared to a pure solvent?
शुद्ध विलायक की तुलना में एक आदर्श विलयन का 'हिमांक' क्या होता है?
e: This is called 'Depression in Freezing Point'. / इसे 'हिमांक में अवनमन' कहा जाता है।

q:
Which substance acts as a 'Strong Oxidizing Agent' in the laboratory?
प्रयोगशाला में कौन सा पदार्थ एक 'प्रबल ऑक्सीकारक' के रूप में कार्य करता है?
e: It readily accepts electrons and gets reduced. / यह आसानी से इलेक्ट्रॉन ग्रहण करता है और अपचयित हो जाता है।

q:
What is 'Raoult's Law' for a solution containing non-volatile solute?
अवाष्पशील विलेय वाले विलयन के लिए 'राउल्ट का नियम' क्या है?
e: Vapor pressure is proportional to mole fraction of solvent. / वाष्प दाब विलायक के मोल अंश के समानुपाती होता है।

q:
The sum of oxidation numbers of all atoms in a 'Neutral' molecule is:
एक 'उदासीन' अणु में सभी परमाणुओं की ऑक्सीकरण संख्याओं का योग होता है:
e: Total charge on a neutral compound is zero. / एक उदासीन यौगिक पर कुल आवेश शून्य होता है।

q:
What is the 'Ebullioscopic Constant' ($K_b$) also known as?
'एबुलियोस्कोपिक स्थिरांक' ($K_b$) को और किस नाम से जाना जाता है?
o: Molal Boiling Point Elevation Constant | मोलल क्वथनांक उन्नयन स्थिरांक
e: Unit is $K \cdot kg/mol$. / इकाई $K \cdot kg/mol$ है।

q:
In the reaction $MnO_4^- + 5e^- ightarrow Mn^{2+}$, this process is:
अभिक्रिया $MnO_4^- + 5e^- ightarrow Mn^{2+}$ में, यह प्रक्रिया है:
e: Gain of electrons is reduction. / इलेक्ट्रॉनों का ग्रहण करना अपचयन है।

q:
What is 'Abnormal Molar Mass' when solute associates (joins) in a solution?
जब विलेय विलयन में संयोजित (जुड़ता) होता है तो 'असामान्य मोलर द्रव्यमान' क्या होता है?
o: Higher than normal | सामान्य से अधिक
e: Example: Acetic acid dimers in benzene. / उदाहरण: बेंजीन में एसिटिक एसिड के डाइमर।

q:
Which 'Non-metal' is the strongest oxidizing agent?
कौन सी 'अधातु' सबसे प्रबल ऑक्सीकारक है?
e: Fluorine has the highest tendency to gain electrons. / फ्लोरीन में इलेक्ट्रॉन ग्रहण करने की प्रवृत्ति सबसे अधिक होती है।

q:
What is the 'Cryoscopic Constant' ($K_f$) related to?
'क्रायोस्कोपिक स्थिरांक' ($K_f$) किससे संबंधित है?
e: Also called Molal Depression Constant. / इसे मोलल अवनमन स्थिरांक भी कहा जाता है।

q:
What is the 'Oxidation State' of Chlorine in $HClO_4$?
$HClO_4$ में क्लोरीन की 'ऑक्सीकरण अवस्था' क्या है?
e: $1 + x + 4(-2) = 0 \Rightarrow x = +7$. / इसकी गणना $+1 + x - 8 = 0$ के रूप में की जाती है।

q:
An 'Isotonic' solution has the same:
'आइसोटोनिक' विलयन में समान होता है:
e: No net flow of water across semi-permeable membrane. / अर्ध-पारगम्य झिल्ली में पानी का कोई शुद्ध प्रवाह नहीं होता।

q:
Which reaction is used in 'Dry Cells' for oxidation at Zinc anode?
'शुष्क सेल' में जस्ता एनोड पर ऑक्सीकरण के लिए कौन सी अभिक्रिया होती है?
e: Zinc metal loses electrons to produce current. / जस्ता धातु धारा उत्पन्न करने के लिए इलेक्ट्रॉनों का त्याग करती है।

q:
What is 'Relative Lowering of Vapor Pressure'?
'वाष्प दाब का आपेक्षिक अवनमन' क्या है?
e: It is equal to the mole fraction of the solute ($X_{solute}$). / यह विलेय के मोल अंश ($X_{solute}$) के बराबर होता है।

q:
What is the 'Oxidation Number' of Iron in $Fe_3O_4$?
$Fe_3O_4$ में लोहे की 'ऑक्सीकरण संख्या' क्या है?
e: It is a mixed oxide of FeO and $Fe_2O_3$. / यह FeO और $Fe_2O_3$ का एक मिश्रित ऑक्साइड है।

q:
The value of 'i' (Van't Hoff factor) for association is always:
संयोजन (association) के लिए 'i' (वैन हॉफ फैक्टर) का मान हमेशा होता है:
e: Because the number of particles decreases. / क्योंकि कणों की संख्या कम हो जाती है।

q:
Which is a 'Non-Redox' reaction?
कौन सी 'नॉन-रेडॉक्स' अभिक्रिया है?
e: Acid-Base neutralization involves no change in oxidation states. / अम्ल-क्षार उदासीनीकरण में ऑक्सीकरण अवस्थाओं में कोई परिवर्तन नहीं होता है।

q:
What is the 'Osmotic Pressure' of a 1M sugar solution at 273 K?
273 K पर 1M चीनी के घोल का 'परासरण दाब' क्या है?
e: Using $\pi = CRT = 1 \times 0.0821 \times 273 \approx 22.4$. / $\pi = CRT$ का उपयोग करके।

q:
What is the oxidation number of Cr in $K_2Cr_2O_7$?
$K_2Cr_2O_7$ में Cr की ऑक्सीकरण संख्या क्या है?
e: $2(+1) + 2x + 7(-2) = 0 \Rightarrow 2x = 12 \Rightarrow x = +6$. / इसकी गणना $+2 + 2x - 14 = 0$ के रूप में की जाती है।

q:
Which property is used to determine the 'Purity' of water using electrical conductivity?
विद्युत चालकता का उपयोग करके पानी की 'शुद्धता' निर्धारित करने के लिए किस गुण का उपयोग किया जाता है?
e: Pure water has very low conductance. / शुद्ध जल की चालकता बहुत कम होती है।

q:
The 'Lowering of Vapor Pressure' depends on:
'वाष्प दाब का अवनमन' निर्भर करता है:
o: Concentration of solute | विलेय की सांद्रता
e: It is a colligative property. / यह एक अणुसंख्य गुणधर्म है।

q:
What is the oxidation number of Nitrogen in $NH_3$?
$NH_3$ में नाइट्रोजन की ऑक्सीकरण संख्या क्या है?

Which solution will have the 'Highest' boiling point?
किस विलयन का क्वथनांक 'उच्चतम' होगा?
e: $BaCl_2$ produces 3 ions, giving the highest elevation. / $BaCl_2$ 3 आयन पैदा करता है, जिससे उच्चतम उन्नयन होता है।

q:
What is 'Reduction' in terms of oxidation number?
ऑक्सीकरण संख्या के संदर्भ में 'अपचयन' क्या है?
o: Decrease in oxidation number | ऑक्सीकरण संख्या में कमी
e: Example: $Fe^{3+} + e^- ightarrow Fe^{2+}$. / उदाहरण: $Fe^{3+}$ से $Fe^{2+}$ बनना।

q:
Which property is 'Directly Proportional' to Molality?
कौन सा गुण मोललता के 'सीधे समानुपाती' है?
o: Elevation in boiling point | क्वथनांक में उन्नयन
e: $\Delta T_b = K_b \cdot m$. / $\Delta T_b = K_b \cdot m$।

q:
What is the 'Oxidation State' of Sulfur in $S_8$ ring?
$S_8$ वलय में सल्फर की 'ऑक्सीकरण अवस्था' क्या है?
e: Elemental form always has zero oxidation state. / तात्विक रूप में हमेशा शून्य ऑक्सीकरण अवस्था होती है।

q:
What is the value of 'i' for Glucose?
ग्लूकोज के लिए 'i' का मान क्या है?
e: Glucose is a non-electrolyte and does not dissociate. / ग्लूकोज एक गैर-विद्युतअपघट्य है और वियोजित नहीं होता है।

q:
In a Redox reaction, the 'Oxidizing Agent' itself gets:
एक रेडॉक्स अभिक्रिया में, 'ऑक्सीकारक' स्वयं हो जाता है:
e: It gains electrons from others. / यह दूसरों से इलेक्ट्रॉन ग्रहण करता है।

q:
What happens to the 'Boiling Point' of a liquid at high altitudes (mountains)?
अधिक ऊँचाई (पहाड़ों) पर तरल के 'क्वथनांक' का क्या होता है?
e: Atmospheric pressure is lower, so it boils at a lower temp. / वायुमंडलीय दबाव कम होता है, इसलिए यह कम तापमान पर उबलता है।

q:
What is the 'Oxidation Number' of Oxygen in Peroxides like $H_2O_2$?
$H_2O_2$ जैसे पेरोक्साइड में ऑक्सीजन की 'ऑक्सीकरण संख्या' क्या है?
e: In peroxides, Oxygen-Oxygen single bond is present. / पेरोक्साइड में ऑक्सीजन-ऑक्सीजन एकल बंध होता है।

q:
Which property is 'Inversely Proportional' to the molar mass of the solute?
कौन सा गुण विलेय के मोलर द्रव्यमान के 'व्युत्क्रमानुपाती' है?
o: All of the above | उपरोक्त सभी
e: All colligative properties are used to calculate molar mass. / सभी अणुसंख्य गुणधर्मों का उपयोग मोलर द्रव्यमान की गणना के लिए किया जाता है।

q:
The reaction $2H_2 + O_2 ightarrow 2H_2O$ is a:
अभिक्रिया $2H_2 + O_2 ightarrow 2H_2O$ एक है:
e: Hydrogen is oxidized (0 to +1) and Oxygen is reduced (0 to -2). / हाइड्रोजन का ऑक्सीकरण और ऑक्सीजन का अपचयन होता है।

q:
What is 'Reverse Osmosis' (RO) pressure compared to osmotic pressure?
परासरण दाब की तुलना में 'रिवर्स ऑस्मोसिस' (RO) दाब क्या होता है?
e: Applied pressure must be greater than osmotic pressure. / लगाया गया दाब परासरण दाब से अधिक होना चाहिए।

q:
Which is an example of 'Disproportionation' reaction?
'विषमानुपातन' (Disproportionation) अभिक्रिया का उदाहरण कौन सा है?
e: Oxygen in $H_2O_2$ is both oxidized and reduced. / $H_2O_2$ में ऑक्सीजन का ऑक्सीकरण और अपचयन दोनों होता है।

q:
What is 'Oxidation' in terms of electron transfer?
इलेक्ट्रॉन स्थानांतरण के संदर्भ में 'ऑक्सीकरण' क्या है?
o: Loss of electrons | इलेक्ट्रॉनों की हानि
e: OIL RIG: Oxidation Is Loss, Reduction Is Gain. / ऑक्सीकरण का अर्थ है इलेक्ट्रॉनों का त्याग।

q:
What is the 'Oxidation Number' of Oxygen in $O_2$ molecule?
$O_2$ अणु में ऑक्सीजन की 'ऑक्सीकरण संख्या' क्या है?
e: Oxidation number of an element in its free state is always zero. / मुक्त अवस्था में किसी तत्व की ऑक्सीकरण संख्या हमेशा शून्य होती है।

q:
Which property depends only on the 'Number' of solute particles and not their nature?
कौन सा गुण केवल विलेय कणों की 'संख्या' पर निर्भर करता है, न कि उनकी प्रकृति पर?
e: Includes Osmotic pressure, elevation of boiling point, etc. / इसमें परासरण दाब, क्वथनांक में उन्नयन आदि शामिल हैं।

q:
What happens to the 'Vapor Pressure' of a solvent when a non-volatile solute is added?
जब एक अवाष्पशील विलेय मिलाया जाता है तो विलायक के 'वाष्प दाब' का क्या होता है?
e: Solute particles occupy the surface area, reducing evaporation. / विलेय के कण सतह घेर लेते हैं, जिससे वाष्पीकरण कम हो जाता है।

q:
What is the 'Oxidation Number' of Mn in $KMnO_4$?
$KMnO_4$ में Mn की 'ऑक्सीकरण संख्या' क्या है?
e: $+1 + x + 4(-2) = 0 \Rightarrow x = +7$. / इसकी गणना $+1 + x - 8 = 0$ के रूप में की जाती है।

q:
Which of the following is NOT a colligative property?
निम्नलिखित में से कौन सा अणुसंख्य गुणधर्म नहीं है?
e: Surface tension is a property of the liquid itself, not dependent on particle count. / पृष्ठ तनाव तरल का अपना गुण है, कणों की संख्या पर निर्भर नहीं।

q:
What is a 'Reducing Agent'?
'अपचायक' (Reducing Agent) क्या है?
o: Donates electrons | इलेक्ट्रॉन दान करता है
e: A reducing agent gets oxidized itself while reducing others. / अपचायक दूसरों का अपचयन करते हुए स्वयं ऑक्सीकृत हो जाता है।

q:
Why is 'Salt' spread on icy roads in cold countries?
ठंडे देशों में बर्फीली सड़कों पर 'नमक' क्यों छिड़का जाता है?
o: To lower the freezing point of water | पानी के हिमांक को कम करने के लिए
e: Salt causes 'Depression in Freezing Point', melting the ice at lower temps. / नमक 'हिमांक में अवनमन' पैदा करता है, जिससे बर्फ पिघल जाती है।

q:
What is the 'Oxidation State' of Carbon in $CO_2$?
$CO_2$ में कार्बन की 'ऑक्सीकरण अवस्था' क्या है?

Which instrument is used to measure 'Osmotic Pressure'?
'परासरण दाब' मापने के लिए किस उपकरण का उपयोग किया जाता है?
o: Berkeley and Hartley's apparatus | बर्कले और हार्टले का उपकरण
e: It is a standard method for determining osmotic pressure of solutions. / यह विलयनों के परासरण दाब को निर्धारित करने की एक मानक विधि है।

q:
A reaction in which both oxidation and reduction occur simultaneously is called:
वह अभिक्रिया जिसमें ऑक्सीकरण और अपचयन दोनों एक साथ होते हैं, कहलाती है:
e: Reduction + Oxidation = Redox. / अपचयन + ऑक्सीकरण = रेडॉक्स।

q:
What is the 'Van't Hoff Factor' (i) for $NaCl$ in water (assuming 100% dissociation)?
पानी में $NaCl$ के लिए 'वैन हॉफ फैक्टर' (i) क्या है (100% वियोजन मानते हुए)?
e: $NaCl \rightarrow Na^+ + Cl^-$ (total 2 particles). / $NaCl$ दो कणों में टूट जाता है।

q:
What is the oxidation number of Hydrogen in 'Metal Hydrides' like $NaH$?
$NaH$ जैसे 'धातु हाइड्राइड' में हाइड्रोजन की ऑक्सीकरण संख्या क्या है?
e: Hydrogen usually has +1, but in metal hydrides, it is -1. / हाइड्रोजन आमतौर पर +1 होता है, लेकिन धातु हाइड्राइड में यह -1 होता है।

q:
The 'Elevation in Boiling Point' is represented by which formula?
'क्वथनांक में उन्नयन' को किस सूत्र द्वारा दर्शाया जाता है?
o: $\Delta T_b = K_b \cdot m$ | $\Delta T_b = K_b \cdot m$
e: Here m is molality and $K_b$ is ebullioscopic constant. / यहाँ m मोललता है और $K_b$ मोलल उन्नयन स्थिरांक है।

q:
In the reaction $Zn + Cu^{2+} ightarrow Zn^{2+} + Cu$, which species is 'Reduced'?
अभिक्रिया $Zn + Cu^{2+} ightarrow Zn^{2+} + Cu$ में, किस प्रजाति का 'अपचयन' होता है?
e: Cu2+ gains two electrons to become Cu metal. / Cu2+ दो इलेक्ट्रॉन ग्रहण करके Cu धातु बन जाता है।

q:
Which colligative property is used to find the 'Molar Mass' of polymers and proteins?
पॉलिमर और प्रोटीन का 'मोलर द्रव्यमान' ज्ञात करने के लिए किस अणुसंख्य गुणधर्म का उपयोग किया जाता है?
e: It provides measurable values even at very low concentrations. / यह बहुत कम सांद्रता पर भी मापने योग्य मान प्रदान करता है।

q:
What is the 'Oxidation Number' of Sulfur in $H_2SO_4$?
$H_2SO_4$ में सल्फर की 'ऑक्सीकरण संख्या' क्या है?

What is the 'Freezing Point' of an ideal solution compared to a pure solvent?
शुद्ध विलायक की तुलना में एक आदर्श विलयन का 'हिमांक' क्या होता है?
e: This is called 'Depression in Freezing Point'. / इसे 'हिमांक में अवनमन' कहा जाता है।

q:
Which substance acts as a 'Strong Oxidizing Agent' in the laboratory?
प्रयोगशाला में कौन सा पदार्थ एक 'प्रबल ऑक्सीकारक' के रूप में कार्य करता है?
e: It readily accepts electrons and gets reduced. / यह आसानी से इलेक्ट्रॉन ग्रहण करता है और अपचयित हो जाता है।

q:
What is 'Raoult's Law' for a solution containing non-volatile solute?
अवाष्पशील विलेय वाले विलयन के लिए 'राउल्ट का नियम' क्या है?
e: Vapor pressure is proportional to mole fraction of solvent. / वाष्प दाब विलायक के मोल अंश के समानुपाती होता है।

q:
The sum of oxidation numbers of all atoms in a 'Neutral' molecule is:
एक 'उदासीन' अणु में सभी परमाणुओं की ऑक्सीकरण संख्याओं का योग होता है:
e: Total charge on a neutral compound is zero. / एक उदासीन यौगिक पर कुल आवेश शून्य होता है।

q:
What is the 'Ebullioscopic Constant' ($K_b$) also known as?
'एबुलियोस्कोपिक स्थिरांक' ($K_b$) को और किस नाम से जाना जाता है?
o: Molal Boiling Point Elevation Constant | मोलल क्वथनांक उन्नयन स्थिरांक
e: Unit is $K \cdot kg/mol$. / इकाई $K \cdot kg/mol$ है।

q:
In the reaction $MnO_4^- + 5e^- ightarrow Mn^{2+}$, this process is:
अभिक्रिया $MnO_4^- + 5e^- ightarrow Mn^{2+}$ में, यह प्रक्रिया है:
e: Gain of electrons is reduction. / इलेक्ट्रॉनों का ग्रहण करना अपचयन है।

q:
What is 'Abnormal Molar Mass' when solute associates (joins) in a solution?
जब विलेय विलयन में संयोजित (जुड़ता) होता है तो 'असामान्य मोलर द्रव्यमान' क्या होता है?
o: Higher than normal | सामान्य से अधिक
e: Example: Acetic acid dimers in benzene. / उदाहरण: बेंजीन में एसिटिक एसिड के डाइमर।

q:
Which 'Non-metal' is the strongest oxidizing agent?
कौन सी 'अधातु' सबसे प्रबल ऑक्सीकारक है?
e: Fluorine has the highest tendency to gain electrons. / फ्लोरीन में इलेक्ट्रॉन ग्रहण करने की प्रवृत्ति सबसे अधिक होती है।

q:
What is the 'Cryoscopic Constant' ($K_f$) related to?
'क्रायोस्कोपिक स्थिरांक' ($K_f$) किससे संबंधित है?
e: Also called Molal Depression Constant. / इसे मोलल अवनमन स्थिरांक भी कहा जाता है।

q:
What is the 'Oxidation State' of Chlorine in $HClO_4$?
$HClO_4$ में क्लोरीन की 'ऑक्सीकरण अवस्था' क्या है?
e: $1 + x + 4(-2) = 0 \Rightarrow x = +7$. / इसकी गणना $+1 + x - 8 = 0$ के रूप में की जाती है।

q:
An 'Isotonic' solution has the same:
'आइसोटोनिक' विलयन में समान होता है:
e: No net flow of water across semi-permeable membrane. / अर्ध-पारगम्य झिल्ली में पानी का कोई शुद्ध प्रवाह नहीं होता।

q:
Which reaction is used in 'Dry Cells' for oxidation at Zinc anode?
'शुष्क सेल' में जस्ता एनोड पर ऑक्सीकरण के लिए कौन सी अभिक्रिया होती है?
e: Zinc metal loses electrons to produce current. / जस्ता धातु धारा उत्पन्न करने के लिए इलेक्ट्रॉनों का त्याग करती है।

q:
What is 'Relative Lowering of Vapor Pressure'?
'वाष्प दाब का आपेक्षिक अवनमन' क्या है?
e: It is equal to the mole fraction of the solute ($X_{solute}$). / यह विलेय के मोल अंश ($X_{solute}$) के बराबर होता है।

q:
What is the 'Oxidation Number' of Iron in $Fe_3O_4$?
$Fe_3O_4$ में लोहे की 'ऑक्सीकरण संख्या' क्या है?
e: It is a mixed oxide of FeO and $Fe_2O_3$. / यह FeO और $Fe_2O_3$ का एक मिश्रित ऑक्साइड है।

q:
The value of 'i' (Van't Hoff factor) for association is always:
संयोजन (association) के लिए 'i' (वैन हॉफ फैक्टर) का मान हमेशा होता है:
e: Because the number of particles decreases. / क्योंकि कणों की संख्या कम हो जाती है।

q:
Which is a 'Non-Redox' reaction?
कौन सी 'नॉन-रेडॉक्स' अभिक्रिया है?
e: Acid-Base neutralization involves no change in oxidation states. / अम्ल-क्षार उदासीनीकरण में ऑक्सीकरण अवस्थाओं में कोई परिवर्तन नहीं होता है।

q:
What is the 'Osmotic Pressure' of a 1M sugar solution at 273 K?
273 K पर 1M चीनी के घोल का 'परासरण दाब' क्या है?
e: Using $\pi = CRT = 1 \times 0.0821 \times 273 \approx 22.4$. / $\pi = CRT$ का उपयोग करके।

q:
What is the oxidation number of Cr in $K_2Cr_2O_7$?
$K_2Cr_2O_7$ में Cr की ऑक्सीकरण संख्या क्या है?
e: $2(+1) + 2x + 7(-2) = 0 \Rightarrow 2x = 12 \Rightarrow x = +6$. / इसकी गणना $+2 + 2x - 14 = 0$ के रूप में की जाती है।

q:
Which property is used to determine the 'Purity' of water using electrical conductivity?
विद्युत चालकता का उपयोग करके पानी की 'शुद्धता' निर्धारित करने के लिए किस गुण का उपयोग किया जाता है?
e: Pure water has very low conductance. / शुद्ध जल की चालकता बहुत कम होती है।

q:
The 'Lowering of Vapor Pressure' depends on:
'वाष्प दाब का अवनमन' निर्भर करता है:
o: Concentration of solute | विलेय की सांद्रता
e: It is a colligative property. / यह एक अणुसंख्य गुणधर्म है।

q:
What is the oxidation number of Nitrogen in $NH_3$?
$NH_3$ में नाइट्रोजन की ऑक्सीकरण संख्या क्या है?

Which solution will have the 'Highest' boiling point?
किस विलयन का क्वथनांक 'उच्चतम' होगा?
e: $BaCl_2$ produces 3 ions, giving the highest elevation. / $BaCl_2$ 3 आयन पैदा करता है, जिससे उच्चतम उन्नयन होता है।

q:
What is 'Reduction' in terms of oxidation number?
ऑक्सीकरण संख्या के संदर्भ में 'अपचयन' क्या है?
o: Decrease in oxidation number | ऑक्सीकरण संख्या में कमी
e: Example: $Fe^{3+} + e^- ightarrow Fe^{2+}$. / उदाहरण: $Fe^{3+}$ से $Fe^{2+}$ बनना।

q:
Which property is 'Directly Proportional' to Molality?
कौन सा गुण मोललता के 'सीधे समानुपाती' है?
o: Elevation in boiling point | क्वथनांक में उन्नयन
e: $\Delta T_b = K_b \cdot m$. / $\Delta T_b = K_b \cdot m$।

q:
What is the 'Oxidation State' of Sulfur in $S_8$ ring?
$S_8$ वलय में सल्फर की 'ऑक्सीकरण अवस्था' क्या है?
e: Elemental form always has zero oxidation state. / तात्विक रूप में हमेशा शून्य ऑक्सीकरण अवस्था होती है।

q:
What is the value of 'i' for Glucose?
ग्लूकोज के लिए 'i' का मान क्या है?
e: Glucose is a non-electrolyte and does not dissociate. / ग्लूकोज एक गैर-विद्युतअपघट्य है और वियोजित नहीं होता है।

q:
In a Redox reaction, the 'Oxidizing Agent' itself gets:
एक रेडॉक्स अभिक्रिया में, 'ऑक्सीकारक' स्वयं हो जाता है:
e: It gains electrons from others. / यह दूसरों से इलेक्ट्रॉन ग्रहण करता है।

q:
What happens to the 'Boiling Point' of a liquid at high altitudes (mountains)?
अधिक ऊँचाई (पहाड़ों) पर तरल के 'क्वथनांक' का क्या होता है?
e: Atmospheric pressure is lower, so it boils at a lower temp. / वायुमंडलीय दबाव कम होता है, इसलिए यह कम तापमान पर उबलता है।

q:
What is the 'Oxidation Number' of Oxygen in Peroxides like $H_2O_2$?
$H_2O_2$ जैसे पेरोक्साइड में ऑक्सीजन की 'ऑक्सीकरण संख्या' क्या है?
e: In peroxides, Oxygen-Oxygen single bond is present. / पेरोक्साइड में ऑक्सीजन-ऑक्सीजन एकल बंध होता है।

q:
Which property is 'Inversely Proportional' to the molar mass of the solute?
कौन सा गुण विलेय के मोलर द्रव्यमान के 'व्युत्क्रमानुपाती' है?
o: All of the above | उपरोक्त सभी
e: All colligative properties are used to calculate molar mass. / सभी अणुसंख्य गुणधर्मों का उपयोग मोलर द्रव्यमान की गणना के लिए किया जाता है।

q:
The reaction $2H_2 + O_2 ightarrow 2H_2O$ is a:
अभिक्रिया $2H_2 + O_2 ightarrow 2H_2O$ एक है:
e: Hydrogen is oxidized (0 to +1) and Oxygen is reduced (0 to -2). / हाइड्रोजन का ऑक्सीकरण और ऑक्सीजन का अपचयन होता है।

q:
What is 'Reverse Osmosis' (RO) pressure compared to osmotic pressure?
परासरण दाब की तुलना में 'रिवर्स ऑस्मोसिस' (RO) दाब क्या होता है?
e: Applied pressure must be greater than osmotic pressure. / लगाया गया दाब परासरण दाब से अधिक होना चाहिए।

q:
Which is an example of 'Disproportionation' reaction?
'विषमानुपातन' (Disproportionation) अभिक्रिया का उदाहरण कौन सा है?
e: Oxygen in $H_2O_2$ is both oxidized and reduced. / $H_2O_2$ में ऑक्सीजन का ऑक्सीकरण और अपचयन दोनों होता है।

q:
What is the 'Oxidation State' of Sulfur in $S_8$ ring?
$S_8$ वलय में सल्फर की 'ऑक्सीकरण अवस्था' क्या है?
e: Elemental form always has zero oxidation state. / तात्विक रूप में हमेशा शून्य ऑक्सीकरण अवस्था होती है।

e: Glucose is a non-electrolyte and does not dissociate. / ग्लूकोज एक गैर-विद्युतअपघट्य है और वियोजित नहीं होता है।

एक रेडॉक्स अभिक्रिया में, 'ऑक्सीकारक' स्वयं हो जाता है:
e: It gains electrons from others. / यह दूसरों से इलेक्ट्रॉन ग्रहण करता है।

अधिक ऊँचाई (पहाड़ों) पर तरल के 'क्वथनांक' का क्या होता है?
e: Atmospheric pressure is lower, so it boils at a lower temp. / वायुमंडलीय दबाव कम होता है, इसलिए यह कम तापमान पर उबलता है।

e: In peroxides, Oxygen-Oxygen single bond is present. / पेरोक्साइड में ऑक्सीजन-ऑक्सीजन एकल बंध होता है।

कौन सा गुण विलेय के मोलर द्रव्यमान के 'व्युत्क्रमानुपाती' है?
o: All of the above | उपरोक्त सभी
e: All colligative properties are used to calculate molar mass. / सभी अणुसंख्य गुणधर्मों का उपयोग मोलर द्रव्यमान की गणना के लिए किया जाता है।

q:
The reaction $2H_2 + O_2 ightarrow 2H_2O$ is a:
अभिक्रिया $2H_2 + O_2 ightarrow 2H_2O$ एक है:
e: Hydrogen is oxidized (0 to +1) and Oxygen is reduced (0 to -2). / हाइड्रोजन का ऑक्सीकरण और ऑक्सीजन का अपचयन होता है।

What is 'Reverse Osmosis' (RO) pressure compared to osmotic pressure?
परासरण दाब की तुलना में 'रिवर्स ऑस्मोसिस' (RO) दाब क्या होता है?
e: Applied pressure must be greater than osmotic pressure. / लगाया गया दाब परासरण दाब से अधिक होना चाहिए।

q:
Which is an example of 'Disproportionation' reaction?
'विषमानुपातन' (Disproportionation) अभिक्रिया का उदाहरण कौन सा है?
e: Oxygen in $H_2O_2$ is both oxidized and reduced. / $H_2O_2$ में ऑक्सीजन का ऑक्सीकरण और अपचयन दोनों होता है।

q:
Which gas constant 'R' value is 2 in which unit?
किस इकाई में गैस नियतांक 'R' का मान 2 होता है?
e: Approximately 1.987 cal/K mol. / लगभग 1.987 cal/K mol।

q:
What is 'Lewis Acid' definition?
'लुईस अम्ल' की परिभाषा क्या है?
o: Electron pair acceptor | इलेक्ट्रॉन युग्म ग्राही
e: Example: $BF_3$, $AlCl_3$. / उदाहरण: $BF_3$, $AlCl_3$।

q:
What happens to entropy when a 'Gas expands' into a vacuum?
जब एक 'गैस निर्वात में फैलती है' तो एन्ट्रॉपी का क्या होता है?
e: More space allows for more disorder. / अधिक स्थान अधिक अव्यवस्था की अनुमति देता है।

q:
Which metal is used as 'Radiation Shield' due to its high density?
उच्च घनत्व के कारण किस धातु का उपयोग 'विकिरण ढाल' (Radiation Shield) के रूप में किया जाता है?
e: Lead can effectively block X-rays and Gamma rays. / सीसा प्रभावी रूप से एक्स-रे और गामा किरणों को रोक सकता है।

q:
What is the unit of 'Activity' named after Marie Curie?
मैरी क्यूरी के नाम पर 'एक्टिविटी' की इकाई क्या है?
e: 1 Curie = 37 Giga Becquerels. / 1 क्यूरी = 37 गीगा बेकरेल।

q:
What is the packing efficiency of an 'FCC' unit cell?
एक 'FCC' यूनिट सेल की पैकिंग क्षमता क्या है?
e: Most efficient among the cubic systems. / घनीय प्रणालियों में सबसे कुशल।

q:
What is the packing efficiency of an 'FCC' unit cell?
एक 'FCC' यूनिट सेल की पैकिंग क्षमता क्या है?
e: Most efficient among the cubic systems. / घनीय प्रणालियों में सबसे कुशल।

q:
What is the packing efficiency of an 'FCC' unit cell?
एक 'FCC' यूनिट सेल की पैकिंग क्षमता क्या है?
e: Most efficient among the cubic systems. / घनीय प्रणालियों में सबसे कुशल।

q:
Which 'Catalyst' is used in the manufacture of $H_2SO_4$ by Contact Process?
संपर्क प्रक्रम द्वारा $H_2SO_4$ के निर्माण में किस 'उत्प्रेरक' का उपयोग किया जाता है?
e: It catalyzes the oxidation of $SO_2$ to $SO_3$. / यह $SO_2$ से $SO_3$ के ऑक्सीकरण को उत्प्रेरित करता है।

q:
What is 'CMC' (Critical Micelle Concentration)?
'CMC' (क्रांतिक मिसेल सांद्रता) क्या है?
o: Concentration above which micelles form | सांद्रता जिसके ऊपर मिसेल बनते हैं
e: Micelles only form above this concentration. / मिसेल केवल इस सांद्रता से ऊपर बनते हैं।

q: Which non-metal is used in 'Safety Matches'?
e: Red phosphorus is used on the striking surface of matchboxes because it is less toxic and less reactive than white phosphorus.

q: The process of 'Liquation' is used for refining metals with:
e: The metal melts and flows away from the higher-melting impurities on a sloping hearth.

q: Which gas is released when 'Aluminum' reacts with Sodium Hydroxide ($NaOH$)?
e: Amphoteric metals like Al and Zn react with strong bases to release hydrogen gas.

q: What is the 'Flux' used in the extraction of Copper to remove Iron oxide ($FeO$)?
e: Silica reacts with iron oxide to form iron silicate slag ($FeSiO_3$).

q: Which 'Alloy' is used to make coins and is composed of Copper and Nickel?
e: Cupro-nickel is highly resistant to seawater corrosion and is used for silver-colored coins.

q: What is 'Dead Burnt Plaster'?
e: Formed by heating Gypsum above 200°C; it loses all water and does not 'set' when mixed with water.

q: Which 'Metal' is used in the 'Photoelectric Cell' because it emits electrons easily when exposed to light?
e: Cesium has a very low ionization energy, making it highly sensitive to light.

q: The 'Pitting Corrosion' is most commonly seen in which material?
e: Pitting is a localized form of corrosion that creates small holes in the metal.

q: Which of the following is considered a 'Noble Metal' due to its resistance to oxidation and corrosion?
e: Platinum, along with Gold and Silver, is chemically unreactive and does not easily form oxides in nature.

q: What is the primary cause of 'Minamata Disease' discovered in Japan?
e: Methylmercury bioaccumulation in fish led to severe neurological damage in the local population.

q: Which metal is used in 'Bulletproof' glass and armor plating due to its high strength-to-weight ratio?
e: Titanium is as strong as steel but much lighter, making it ideal for high-performance defense applications.

q: Which disease is caused by chronic 'Cadmium' poisoning, leading to brittle bones and joint pain?
e: Itai-Itai (meaning 'it hurts, it hurts') was caused by cadmium contamination from mining in the Jinzū River.

q: What is 'Misch Metal' primarily composed of?
e: Misch metal (approx 95% Lanthanides) is used in cigarette lighter flints to produce sparks.

q: Which heavy metal is most commonly found in 'Lead Storage Batteries' used in Bihar households?
e: Lead-acid batteries use lead plates and lead dioxide in a sulfuric acid electrolyte.

q: Which metal is known as the 'Strategic Metal' because it is vital for a country's defense and economy?
e: Titanium, Chromium, and Zirconium are called strategic metals due to their use in aerospace and nuclear technology.

q: The 'Plumbism' medical condition refers to the poisoning caused by which metal?
e: Plumbism is another name for lead poisoning, derived from 'Plumbum', the Latin name for Lead.

q: Which element is used as a 'Dopant' in Silicon to create a p-type semiconductor?
e: Boron is a Group 13 element; adding it creates 'holes' in the silicon crystal lattice.

q: What is the main component of 'Fool's Gold'?
e: Pyrite or Iron Sulfide has a metallic luster and brass-yellow hue that mimics real gold.

q: Which metal is used in 'Nuclear Control Rods' to absorb neutrons efficiently?
e: Cadmium and Boron have a high probability of capturing slow neutrons, helping regulate the chain reaction.

q: The 'Hardness' of steel is increased by adding which element during its manufacture?
e: While iron is ductile, adding small amounts of carbon disrupts the lattice, making it much harder.

q: Which metal is found in 'Vitamin B12' (Cyanocobalamin)?
e: Cobalt is the central atom in Vitamin B12, which is essential for red blood cell formation.

q: What is 'White Gold' usually an alloy of?
e: White gold is made by alloying yellow gold with at least one white metal and often plating it with Rhodium.

q: Which non-metal is used in 'Vulcanized' tires to make them heat resistant?
e: Sulfur cross-links the polyisoprene chains in rubber, a process discovered by Charles Goodyear.

q: Which metal is known for having the 'Highest Electrical Conductivity'?
e: Silver is the most conductive, but Copper is more commonly used in wiring due to its lower cost.

q: What is 'Galena' the principal ore of?
e: Galena (PbS) is the most common sulfide ore of lead.

q: Which metal was used in ancient times as a 'Sweetener' (Lead sugar) in wine, leading to widespread poisoning?
e: Lead(II) acetate has a sweet taste but is highly toxic; its use contributed to health issues in the Roman Empire.

q: Which process is used for the mass production of 'Steel' from molten pig iron?
e: The Bessemer process involves blowing air through molten pig iron to oxidize impurities like Carbon and Silicon.

q: In the Blast Furnace, the 'Zone of Reduction' occurs at what temperature range?
e: The top part of the furnace is cooler, where Carbon Monoxide reduces Iron oxides to metallic Iron.

q: What is the chemical composition of 'Stainless Steel'?
e: Chromium (at least 10.5%) provides the corrosion resistance by forming a passive oxide layer.

q: Which gas is known as 'Producer Gas' and is used as fuel in the Open Hearth process?
e: Producer gas is a mixture of Carbon Monoxide and Nitrogen, made by passing air over red-hot coke.

q: The 'L-D Process' (Linz-Donawitz) is a modern method for manufacturing:
e: It is a type of basic oxygen steelmaking where high-purity oxygen is blown onto the surface of molten iron.

q: Which form of Iron is used for making 'Anchors' and 'Chains' because it is tough and fibrous?
e: Wrought iron is the purest commercial iron and is highly resistant to corrosion and mechanical fatigue.

q: What is 'Spiegeleisen'?
o: An alloy of Iron, Manganese, and Carbon
e: It is used in the Bessemer process to re-introduce necessary Carbon and Manganese into the steel.

q: Which reaction represents the 'Zone of Combustion' in the Blast Furnace?
e: At the bottom (tuyeres), coke burns in pre-heated air to provide the massive heat required for the process.

q: What is 'Cast Iron' primarily used for?
e: Cast iron is brittle but has high compressive strength and expands slightly on solidification, making it great for casting.

q: Which element is removed from Pig Iron to convert it into Steel?
e: Pig iron has 3-4% Carbon; Steel requires 0.1-1.5% Carbon to be useful.

q: In the 'Open Hearth Process', the lining of the furnace can be 'Basic' or 'Acidic'. What is a 'Basic' lining made of?
e: Magnesite or Dolomite linings are used to remove acidic impurities like Phosphorus and Sulfur.

q: The 'Slag' produced in the Blast Furnace is used for:
e: Calcium Silicate slag is a valuable byproduct used as a construction aggregate.

q: What is 'Invar' steel used for?
e: Invar (Iron + Nickel) has an extremely low coefficient of thermal expansion.

q: Which type of 'Steel' contains Chromium and Vanadium and is used for making 'Axles'?
e: Alloy steels are designed for high strength, hardness, and wear resistance.

q: The 'Charge' of a Blast Furnace consists of:
e: Usually Haematite (ore), Limestone (flux), and Coke (fuel).

q: What is the 'Nature' of the gas exiting from the top of a Blast Furnace?
e: The 'waste' gas is actually flammable and is used to pre-heat the incoming air blast.

q: Which method of 'Corrosion Prevention' is most suitable for heavy machinery?
e: Moving parts cannot be painted; oil/grease prevents moisture contact while allowing movement.

q: What is 'Tungsten Steel' primarily used for?
e: It maintains its hardness even at high temperatures (red-hardness).

q: In the 'Hall-Heroult' process for Aluminum, what is the role of 'Cryolite' ($Na_3AlF_6$)?
o: To lower the melting point of Alumina
e: Pure alumina melts at over 2000°C; adding cryolite lowers the melting point to about 950°C and increases conductivity.

q: What is the 'Cathode' made of in the electrolytic cell for Aluminum extraction?
e: The carbon lining of the steel tank acts as the cathode where molten Aluminum collects at the bottom.

q: Why do the 'Anodes' in Aluminum extraction need to be replaced periodically?
o: They react with evolved Oxygen to form CO2
e: Oxygen released at the anode reacts with the carbon rods ($C + O_2 ightarrow CO_2$), slowly burning them away.

q: Which ore is concentrated by the 'Froth Floatation' process before extracting Copper?
e: Copper Pyrites ($CuFeS_2$) is a sulfide ore; sulfide ores are specifically concentrated using pine oil and froth.

q: What is 'Copper Matte'?
o: A mixture of Cu2S and FeS
A mixture of CuO and FeO
e: Matte is the intermediate product of smelting copper pyrites in a reverberatory furnace.

q: The 'Bessemer Converter' used in copper metallurgy is used to produce:
e: Blister copper gets its name from the bubbles (blisters) of escaping $SO_2$ gas as it solidifies.

q: Which process is used to purify 'Blister Copper' to 99.9% purity?
e: Anode is impure copper, Cathode is pure copper strip, and the electrolyte is acidified $CuSO_4$.

q: What is the 'Flux' added to remove Iron impurity during copper smelting?
e: Silica reacts with Iron oxide ($FeO$) to form Iron silicate slag ($FeSiO_3$).

q: Which metal is obtained as a 'Byproduct' during the electrolytic refining of Copper?
e: Precious metals settle at the bottom as 'Anode Mud' and are recovered to offset refining costs.

q: What is the common name of the ore 'Malachite'?
e: Malachite is a green basic copper carbonate ($CuCO_3 \cdot Cu(OH)_2$).

q: Which Aluminum alloy is used for making 'Kitchen Utensils' because of its high corrosion resistance?
e: Hindalium is an alloy of Aluminum and Magnesium with small amounts of other metals.

q: What is the percentage of Aluminum in 'Duralumin'?
e: Duralumin is mostly aluminum, made strong and light for engineering uses.

q: The 'Baeyer's Process' is used for:
e: It converts Bauxite into pure Alumina ($Al_2O_3$) by dissolving it in concentrated $NaOH$.

q: In copper extraction, 'Poling' is used to remove:
e: Stirring molten copper with green wood poles releases reducing gases that convert oxide back to metal.

q: What is 'Azurite' chemically?
e: Azurite ($2CuCO_3 \cdot Cu(OH)_2$) is a deep blue copper mineral.

q: Which gas is responsible for the 'Blisters' in Blister Copper?
e: As the copper cools, dissolved $SO_2$ escapes, creating a rough surface.

q: What is 'Ruby Copper'?
e: Cuprite is a red oxide ore of copper, sometimes called Ruby Copper.

q: Aluminum is often used as a 'Reducing Agent' in the extraction of which metals?
e: This is called the 'Goldschmidt Aluminothermic' process, used for metals with high melting points.

q: Which metal is most commonly found as a 'Native Element' (pure form) in Bihar's geological history?
e: Gold is chemically inert and is often found in river sands (placer deposits) as pure metal.

q: The 'Serpeck’s Process' is used to purify bauxite containing which impurity?
e: Serpeck’s process is specifically used when Silica is the major impurity in bauxite.

q: Which metal is the 'Lightest' of all solid elements and is essential for electric vehicle batteries?
e: Lithium (Li) has the lowest density of any metal and a high electrochemical potential, making it ideal for rechargeable batteries.

q: What is the 'Kroll Process' used to extract?
e: The Kroll process involves reducing Titanium Tetrachloride (TiCl4) with Magnesium in an inert atmosphere.

q: Which allotrope of Sulfur is stable at room temperature?
e: Rhombic (α-sulfur) is the most stable form; it converts to Monoclinic (β-sulfur) above 95.6°C.

q: What is the 'Transition Temperature' of Sulfur?
e: This is the temperature at which both Rhombic and Monoclinic forms of sulfur coexist in equilibrium.

q: Which non-metal is used in 'Vulcanization' to make rubber tougher for Bihar's rugged road conditions?
e: Sulfur forms cross-links between polymer chains, preventing the rubber from becoming too soft in heat.

q: What is 'White Phosphorus' stored in to prevent it from catching fire spontaneously?
e: White phosphorus reacts with oxygen even at room temperature, but it does not react with water.

q: Which metal is used in 'Surgical Implants' because it is bio-compatible and does not corrode in the body?
e: Titanium's strength and resistance to body fluids make it the standard for joint replacements and dental implants.

q: Which allotrope of Carbon is used as a 'Dry Lubricant' in heavy machinery?
e: The layers in graphite slide over each other easily due to weak Van der Waals forces between them.

q: What is 'Steel' with a high percentage of Nickel and Chromium called?
e: Stainless steel typically contains 18% Chromium and 8% Nickel to ensure maximum rust resistance.

q: Which metal is used in 'Mobile Phone' capacitors and is often called 'Blood Mineral' due to mining conflicts?
e: Tantalum is essential for miniaturized capacitors in high-end electronics.

q: Which non-metal is used in the 'Haber Process' for synthesizing Ammonia for fertilizers?
e: Nitrogen from the air is reacted with Hydrogen to produce Ammonia ($NH_3$).

q: What is 'Babbitt Metal' primarily used for?
e: It is a tin-based or lead-based alloy with low friction properties, ideal for rotating shafts.

q: Which gas is evolved when 'Zinc' reacts with concentrated Sulfuric Acid?
e: Unlike dilute acid (which gives H2), concentrated H2SO4 acts as an oxidizing agent and produces SO2.

q: The 'Hope’s Apparatus' is related to the study of which property of water?
e: It demonstrates that water has maximum density at 4°C, which is vital for aquatic life in cold climates.

q: Which metal is used to make 'Permanent Magnets' (Alnico)?
e: Alnico is an acronym for Aluminum-Nickel-Cobalt.

q: What is the 'Nature' of the oxide of Aluminum ($Al_2O_3$)?
e: It can react with both acids (forming Aluminum salts) and bases (forming Aluminates).

q: Which element is used as a 'Shield' against X-rays and Gamma rays in hospitals?
e: Lead (Pb) has a high atomic number and density, making it excellent at absorbing high-energy radiation.

q: What is 'Wood's Metal' known for?
o: Having an extremely low melting point
e: It is an alloy that melts at 70°C, used in fire sprinkler systems.

q: Which mineral found in the 'Gaya' and 'Munger' districts of Bihar is a source of Lithium and Beryllium?
e: Lepidolite is a lithium-bearing mica found in the pegmatites of the Bihar mica belt.

q: Which element is known as the 'Metal of Hope' because of its role in future green energy?
e: Lithium is the backbone of the global transition to electric vehicles and renewable energy storage.

q: What is 'Promethium' unique for in the Lanthanide series?
o: It is the only radioactive lanthanide
It is found in gold mines
e: Promethium does not occur naturally on Earth and is produced artificially in nuclear reactors.

q: Which metal is used as a 'Thermometer' fluid for measuring very high temperatures (up to 2000°C)?
e: Gallium has a very wide liquid range (melts at 29.7°C, boils at 2229°C), unlike Mercury which boils much lower.

q: Which 'Rare Earth' element is used to make the strongest permanent magnets (Neo-magnets)?
e: Neodymium-Iron-Boron (NdFeB) magnets are essential for hard drives and electric motors.

q: Which mineral is known as 'Mineral Silk'?
e: Asbestos is a fibrous silicate mineral once widely used for its fire-resistant properties.

q: What is the primary use of 'Pyrolusite' ore?
e: Pyrolusite ($MnO_2$) is the most important ore for producing manganese used in steel alloys.

q: Which metal is used in 'Surgical Staples' because it is highly resistant to body fluids and non-magnetic?
e: Tantalum is extremely inert, making it a favorite for medical implants and staples.

q: Which form of 'Coal' is considered the highest grade with the most Carbon content?
e: Anthracite contains over 90% carbon and burns with the least smoke and highest heat.

q: What is 'Pitchblende' an ore of?
e: Pitchblende ($U_3O_8$) was the ore from which Marie Curie first isolated Radium and Polonium.

q: Which element is used to make 'Yellow' color in fireworks?
e: Sodium salts produce a characteristic golden yellow light in pyrotechnics.

q: Which mineral is famous for its 'Perfect Cleavage' and is found extensively in the Koderma-Gaya belt?
e: Mica can be split into extremely thin, transparent, and flexible sheets.

q: Which heavy metal poisoning leads to 'Blue lines' on the gums (Burton's lines)?
e: Burton's line is a clinical sign of chronic lead poisoning (Plumbism).

q: What is 'Rutile' an ore of?
e: Rutile is a mineral composed primarily of Titanium Dioxide ($TiO_2$).

q: Which element is added to 'Gold' to make it 'Rose Gold'?
e: The pinkish tint comes from a higher copper content in the gold alloy.

q: Which metal is used for 'Silvering' of mirrors?
e: While silver was traditional, modern mirrors often use a thin layer of Aluminum deposited via vacuum.

q: What is the 'Hardest' synthetic material made by man, often used to coat metal drills?
e: Cubic Boron Nitride is nearly as hard as diamond and more thermally stable.

q: Which non-metal is used in 'Vulcanization' but also as a 'Fungicide' in Bihar's orchards?
e: Elemental sulfur is a traditional remedy for powdery mildew on plants.

q: Which metal is known as 'Quick Silver'?
e: Mercury’s rapid flow and silver appearance earned it the name 'hydrargyrum' (water-silver).

q: What is the composition of 'Gun Metal', traditionally used for making cannons and gears?
e: Gun metal is a type of bronze (approx 88% Copper, 10% Tin, 2% Zinc) known for its strength and corrosion resistance.

q: Which alloy is used to make 'Church Bells' due to its high sonority (ringing sound)?
e: Bell metal is a hard alloy of Copper and Tin with a higher tin content than standard bronze.

q: What is the 'Anodic' reaction during the rusting of Iron?
e: At the anodic site, iron atoms lose electrons and oxidize into ferrous ions.

q: Which Noble gas is used in 'MRI Scanners' to keep the superconducting magnets cool?
e: Liquid Helium has the lowest boiling point of any element (4.2 K), making it the ultimate coolant.

q: Which 'Rare Earth' element is used in the manufacturing of 'Glass Polishing' compounds?
e: Cerium oxide is the most efficient polishing agent for precision optical glass and mirrors.

q: What is 'Constantan' (Eureka) alloy used for in physics labs?
e: Constantan (Copper + Nickel) has a resistivity that is constant over a wide range of temperatures.

q: Which metal is used as a 'Scavenger' in metallurgy to remove traces of Oxygen and Nitrogen from molten steel?
e: Titanium reacts readily with these gases to form stable nitrides and oxides that can be removed.

q: Which 'Non-metal' is used in 'Laser' technology (specifically in CO2 lasers)?
e: Carbon dioxide lasers are widely used for industrial cutting and surgical procedures.

q: What is the primary constituent of 'Monel Metal'?
e: Monel is a group of nickel alloys that are highly resistant to corrosion, especially by seawater.

q: Which metal is 'Passivated' by concentrated Nitric Acid ($HNO_3$)?
e: Concentrated $HNO_3$ forms a thin, protective oxide layer on Al and Fe, stopping further reaction.

q: Which element is used to provide 'Red' color in emergency flares and fireworks?
e: Strontium salts emit a brilliant crimson red color in a flame.

q: What is 'Magnalium' composed of?
e: Magnalium is stronger and more corrosion-resistant than pure aluminum; used in balances and optical instruments.

q: Which non-metal is known as the 'Basis of Life' on Earth?
e: Carbon's ability to form long chains (catenation) allows for the complexity of organic molecules.

q: What is 'Type Metal' used in printing presses composed of?
e: Antimony is added because it expands upon cooling, ensuring a sharp impression of the letter mold.

q: Which metal is used in 'Galvanic Cells' as the negative electrode (Anode)?
e: Zinc is more reactive and loses electrons more easily than copper in a typical Daniell cell.

q: What is 'Dutch Metal'?
o: A form of Brass used as imitation gold leaf
e: It is a very malleable brass alloy used for gilding/imitation gold applications.

q: Which element is used as an 'Antiseptic' in the form of Tincture?
e: Tincture of iodine (iodine in alcohol-water) is a common skin antiseptic.

q: Which metal was historically called 'Wolfram'?
e: The chemical symbol for Tungsten is 'W', derived from its German name Wolfram.

q: What is 'Stellite' used for?
e: Stellite is a range of cobalt-chromium alloys designed for wear resistance and hardness at high temperatures.

q: Which metalloid is the most abundant in the Earth's crust and is the primary component of 'Sand'?
e: Silicon makes up nearly 28% of the Earth's crust, usually in the form of Silica ($SiO_2$) or silicates.

q: What is the 'Color' of Hydrated Copper(II) ions in an aqueous solution?
e: The $[Cu(H_2O)_6]^{2+}$ complex absorbs red-orange light and reflects blue light.

q: Which element is used to 'Dope' Germanium to make it an n-type semiconductor?
e: Arsenic is pentavalent (5 valence electrons); adding it provides an extra 'free' electron to the lattice.

q: What is the characteristic color of 'Potassium Permanganate' ($KMnO_4$) crystals?
e: The intense purple color is due to 'Charge Transfer' from Oxygen to Manganese.

q: Which metalloid is highly 'Toxic' and was historically used as a poison (often called 'Inheritance Powder')?
e: Arsenic compounds are potent cellular poisons and are a major groundwater pollutant in parts of Bihar.

q: What color do 'Ferrous' ($Fe^{2+}$) salts usually exhibit in solid or solution form?
e: Light green crystals of $FeSO_4 \cdot 7H_2O$ are a classic example of ferrous salts.

q: Which 'Transition Metal' is the only one that is a liquid at room temperature?
e: Mercury is a d-block transition element with a very low melting point (-38.8°C).

q: What is 'Boric Acid' used for in households?
e: It is a weak acid of the metalloid Boron, used to kill cockroaches and as a mild eyewash.

q: Which element provides the 'Green' color to fireworks?
e: Barium chloride is commonly used to produce a brilliant green flame in pyrotechnics.

q: What is the 'Nature' of the oxide of Antimony ($Sb_2O_3$)?
e: As a metalloid, Antimony's oxide shows both acidic and basic characteristics.

q: Which transition metal ion is 'Colorless' in aqueous solution?
e: Zinc ($Zn^{2+}$) has a completely filled 3d subshell ($3d^{10}$); with no d-d transitions possible, it is colorless.

q: What is 'Pyrex' glass made of to make it heat-resistant?
e: Borosilicate glass has a very low coefficient of thermal expansion, so it doesn't crack when heated.

q: Which element is used to make 'Blue' colored glass?
e: Cobalt oxide is added to molten glass to give it a deep, rich blue hue.

q: What is 'Yellow Phosphorus' chemically identical to?
e: White phosphorus turns yellow upon exposure to light, hence it is often called yellow phosphorus.

q: Which transition metal is essential for 'Vitamin B12'?
e: Cobalt is the central metal ion in the corrin ring of Vitamin B12 (Cobalamin).

q: What is the 'Oxidation State' of Iron in the Brown Ring complex used to test for Nitrates?
e: In $[Fe(H_2O)_5(NO)]^{2+}$, iron is in the unusual +1 oxidation state.

q: Which metalloid is used in 'Photocopiers' and 'Solar Cells' because it is a photoconductor?
e: Selenium's electrical conductivity increases significantly when exposed to light.

q: What color is 'Potassium Dichromate' ($K_2Cr_2O_7$) in acidic solution?
e: Dichromate ions are orange; if they are reduced to $Cr^{3+}$, they turn green.

q: Which element is used to 'Galvanize' steel but also used in 'Dry Cell' batteries?
e: Zinc forms the outer casing (the anode) of a standard Leclanché dry cell battery.

q: Which of the following transition metal ions is 'Paramagnetic' due to the presence of unpaired electrons?
e: Fe3+ has 5 unpaired electrons in its 3d subshell, making it strongly attracted to magnetic fields.

q: What is 'White Vitriol' chemically?
e: Zinc Sulfate Heptahydrate is known as White Vitriol and is used in eye drops and as a preservative.

q: Which alloy is used to make 'Precision Weights' and 'Standard Meters' because it doesn't expand with heat?
e: Invar is an alloy of Iron and Nickel (36%) with a near-zero coefficient of thermal expansion.

q: What is 'Philosopher's Wool'?
e: When Zinc is burned in air, it forms a white, wool-like powder of Zinc Oxide.

q: Which metal is used in 'Type Metal' along with Lead and Tin to make the alloy expand on cooling?
e: Antimony expands when it solidifies, ensuring that the molten metal fills every corner of the printing mold.

q: What is 'Lithopone', used as a white pigment in paints?
e: Lithopone is a mixture of Barium Sulfate and Zinc Sulfide, valued for its high opacity.

q: Which 'Lead' compound is known as 'Red Lead' or 'Sindoor'?
e: Triplumbic Tetroxide ($Pb_3O_4$) is a bright red/orange powder used in primers and traditional pigments.

q: What is the 'Nature' of Zinc Oxide ($ZnO$)?
e: Like Aluminum oxide, Zinc oxide reacts with both acids and strong bases (forming Zincates).

q: Which metal is used in 'Solder' to join pipes or wires, having the lowest melting point among common alloys?
e: Solder (Pb + Sn) melts at a lower temperature than either pure metal, protecting the components from heat damage.

q: What is 'Sugar of Lead'?
e: Lead(II) Acetate is a sweet-tasting but highly toxic white crystalline salt.

q: Which element is used to 'Desilverize' Lead in the Parkes Process?
e: Silver is more soluble in molten Zinc than in molten Lead, allowing it to be skimmed off the top.

q: What is the primary use of 'Zinc Phosphide'?
e: Zinc phosphide reacts with stomach acid to release toxic phosphine gas, killing the pest.

q: Which 'Transition Metal' is found in the center of the Hemoglobin molecule?
e: Iron in the +2 state is responsible for binding and transporting Oxygen in the blood.

q: What color is 'Anhydrous' Ferrous Sulfate?
e: Ferrous sulfate loses its 7 water molecules and its green color upon heating, becoming a white powder.

q: Which metal is used for 'Silvering' of high-quality laboratory mirrors?
e: Silver provides the highest reflectivity, though aluminum is used for consumer-grade mirrors.

q: What is 'White Lead' chemically?
e: Basic Lead Carbonate was historically used as a white paint pigment but is now banned due to toxicity.

q: Which gas is used in 'Neon' tubes to produce a Blue-Green light?
e: While pure Neon is red, adding Mercury vapor and Argon creates the blue-green spectrum.

q: What is 'Litharge'?
e: Litharge is the yellow/reddish-yellow oxide of lead used in glass and ceramics.

q: Which transition metal is used as a 'Catalyst' in the Haber Process for the synthesis of Ammonia?
e: Finely divided Iron is the catalyst, while Molybdenum acts as a 'Promoter' to increase its efficiency.

q: What is the 'Catalyst' used in the Contact Process to convert $SO_2$ to $SO_3$?
e: Vanadium Pentoxide ($V_2O_5$) is now preferred over Platinum because it is cheaper and less easily poisoned.

q: Which Noble gas is used in 'Deep Sea Diving' mixed with Oxygen to prevent 'the bends' (Decompression sickness)?
e: Helium is much less soluble in blood than Nitrogen, preventing painful gas bubbles during ascent.

q: The 'Ostwald Process' for manufacturing Nitric Acid uses which metal as a catalyst?
e: The catalytic oxidation of Ammonia to Nitric Oxide ($NO$) occurs on the surface of this precious metal gauze.

q: Which Noble gas is 'Radioactive' and used in the treatment of Cancer (Radiotherapy)?
e: Radon ($Rn$) is a decay product of Radium and emits alpha particles used to treat certain tumors.

q: What is the first 'Noble Gas Compound' ever synthesized?
e: Neil Bartlett synthesized Xenon Hexafluoroplatinate in 1962, proving noble gases weren't totally inert.

q: Which transition metal is used in 'Vegetable Ghee' production from oils?
e: Finely divided Nickel catalyzes the addition of Hydrogen to unsaturated fats (Hydrogenation).

q: What is 'Ziegler-Natta' catalyst used for?
e: It is a mixture of Titanium Tetrachloride and Triethylaluminum, allowing for plastic production at low pressure.

q: Which Noble gas provides a 'Golden Yellow' light in discharge tubes?
e: Helium produces a yellow light; Neon is red, and Argon is typically blue-violet.

q: What is the 'Geometry' of Xenon Tetrafluoride ($XeF_4$)?
e: With 4 bond pairs and 2 lone pairs, the molecule adopts a square planar shape to minimize repulsion.

q: Which metal is used in 'Catalytic Converters' of cars to reduce toxic exhaust emissions?
e: These noble metals oxidize Carbon Monoxide to $CO_2$ and reduce Nitrogen oxides to $N_2$.

q: Which element is added to 'Argon' in light bulbs to increase the life of the filament?
e: Bulbs are filled with a mixture of Argon and Nitrogen to prevent the evaporation of the tungsten filament.

q: What is the oxidation state of 'Xenon' in $XeO_3$?
e: Oxygen is -2; with three oxygens, Xenon must be +6 to balance the charge.

q: Which transition metal ion gives 'Amethyst' its characteristic purple color?
e: The purple color in quartz (Amethyst) is due to irradiation of trivalent iron ($Fe^{3+}$) impurities.

q: Which gas is used in 'Arc Welding' to provide an inert atmosphere?
e: Argon prevents the hot metal from reacting with oxygen and nitrogen in the air.

q: What is 'Clathrate' compound?
o: A cage-like structure where one molecule traps another
e: Noble gases like Argon and Krypton can be trapped in the crystal lattices of water or quinol.

q: Which metal is used as a 'Catalyst' in the 'Decomposition of Potassium Chlorate' to produce Oxygen?
e: Adding $MnO_2$ allows the reaction to happen at a much lower temperature.

q: Which Noble gas is found in the highest concentration in the 'Earth's Atmosphere'?
e: Argon makes up about 0.93% of the air, significantly more than $CO_2$.

q: What is the primary cause of 'Lanthanoid Contraction' in the f-block elements?
e: The 4f electrons are diffuse and do not shield the outer electrons effectively from the increasing nuclear charge, causing a steady decrease in size.

q: Which of the following ions will have the highest 'Magnetic Moment'?
e: Fe3+ has 5 unpaired electrons. Using $\mu = \sqrt{n(n+2)}$, $\sqrt{5(7)} = \sqrt{35} \approx 5.92$ BM.

q: What is the 'Main Ore' of Tin (Sn)?
e: Cassiterite, also known as tinstone, is the heavy oxide ore from which Tin is extracted by reduction with carbon.

q: Which metal is extracted from the ore 'Wolframite'?
e: Wolframite is a double tungstate of Iron and Manganese; it is the primary source of Tungsten.

q: Which Lanthanoid element is widely used in making 'Laser' crystals (like Nd:YAG)?
e: Neodymium-doped Yttrium Aluminum Garnet is the most common solid-state laser medium.

q: What is the 'Oxidation State' shown by Cerium (Ce) that makes it a powerful oxidizing agent?
e: While +3 is common for lanthanoids, Ce can reach +4, which is very stable due to an empty f-shell.

q: Which process is used to separate 'Tin' from 'Iron Tungstate' (Wolframite) impurities?
e: Wolframite is magnetic, while Cassiterite (Tin ore) is non-magnetic, allowing for easy separation.

q: What is 'Misch Metal' used for in the defense and automotive industry?
e: It is an alloy of Rare Earth metals that produces sparks easily when struck.

q: The 'Spin-only' magnetic moment of $Zn^{2+}$ ion is:
e: Zn2+ has a $3d^{10}$ configuration with zero unpaired electrons ($n=0$).

q: Which 'Heavy Metal' is found in the ore 'Stibnite'?
e: Stibnite ($Sb_2S_3$) is the principal sulfide ore of Antimony.

q: Which element is used to 'Harden' Lead for making bullets and batteries?
e: Antimony increases the hardness and mechanical strength of lead alloys.

q: What is the 'Self-reduction' reaction for the extraction of Lead (Pb)?
e: During roasting, some PbS is converted to PbO/PbSO4, which then reacts with the remaining PbS to give Lead metal.

q: Which Lanthanoid is used as a 'Neutron Absorber' in nuclear reactors?
e: These elements have a very high 'capture cross-section' for thermal neutrons.

q: The 'Density' of transition metals generally increases across a period. Which transition metal is the 'Lightest'?
e: Scandium (Z=21) has the lowest density among the 3d transition series elements.

q: Which metal is used to make the 'Target' in an X-ray tube?
e: Tungsten is used because of its high atomic number (efficient X-ray production) and high melting point.

q: What is 'White Tin' and 'Grey Tin' an example of?
e: Tin shows allotropy; Grey tin (α) is stable at low temperatures, while White tin (β) is stable at room temperature.

q: Which element is responsible for 'Tin Pest' (crumbling of tin objects in cold climates)?
o: The conversion of White Tin to Grey Tin
e: Below 13.2°C, white tin slowly changes to brittle grey tin, causing the object to disintegrate.

q: Which 'Rare Earth' element is used in the screens of Color Televisions and Monitors to produce the 'Red' phosphor?
e: Europium compounds are essential for the red color in cathode ray and plasma displays.

q: What is the result of 'Lanthanoid Contraction' on the 4d and 5d series elements?
o: Zirconium (4d) and Hafnium (5d) have almost identical radii
e: Due to the contraction, Zr and Hf have nearly identical sizes (chemical twins), making them very hard to separate.

q: Which non-metal is used to 'Degas' or remove impurities from molten steel in the Bessemer process?
e: These elements react with dissolved oxygen to form slag, preventing blowholes in the steel.

q: What type of 'Bonding' is present in Metal Carbonyls like $Ni(CO)_4$?
e: Synergic bonding involves the metal donating electrons from its d-orbitals back into the empty pi-antibonding orbitals of CO.

q: What is the primary constituent of 'Monel Metal', used in marine engineering?
e: Monel (approx 67% Ni, 30% Cu) is extremely resistant to corrosion by seawater and acids.

q: Which alloy is used in 'Jet Engines' because it maintains strength at extremely high temperatures?
e: Inconel is an austenitic nickel-chromium-based superalloy that resists oxidation and pressure at high heat.

q: In an 'Ellingham Diagram', what does a lower position for a metal oxide line indicate?
o: Higher stability and ease of reduction
e: The lower a metal is in the diagram, the more stable its oxide is and the harder it is to reduce with Carbon.

q: Which metal is refined using the 'Mond Process' by forming a volatile carbonyl complex?
e: Nickel reacts with CO at 330-350K to form $Ni(CO)_4$, which is then decomposed to yield 99.9% pure metal.

q: What is 'German Silver' composed of?
e: Despite the name, German Silver contains 0% silver; it is named for its silver-like appearance.

q: Which substance is used to 'Inhibit' the corrosion of iron in car radiators?
e: Corrosion inhibitors form a protective molecular film on the metal surface.

q: What is the 'Effective Atomic Number' (EAN) of Nickel in $Ni(CO)_4$?
e: EAN = Atomic No. (28) + Electrons from ligands (4 x 2) = 36 (Noble gas configuration).

q: Which metal is used to coat the 'Internal Surface' of food cans to prevent food poisoning?
e: Tin is less reactive and non-toxic, whereas Zinc could react with food acids to form poisonous compounds.

q: What is 'Amalgamation' primarily used for in small-scale mining?
e: Mercury dissolves gold to form an amalgam, which is then heated to boil off the mercury and leave the gold.

q: Which element is added to 'Steel' to make it 'Self-lubricating'?
e: Trace amounts of sulfur or lead create microscopic inclusions that act as lubricants during cutting.

q: What is 'White Gold' usually made of?
e: It is an alloy of Gold with a white metal, often finished with a Rhodium plating for extra shine.

q: The 'Vapor Phase Refining' method includes which two famous processes?
e: Both involve converting metal into a volatile compound and then decomposing it.

q: Which metal is used in 'High-pressure Sodium Lamps' (street lights)?
e: Excited sodium vapor produces the characteristic bright yellow-orange light.

q: What is the 'Flux' used in the extraction of Aluminum?
e: They lower the melting point and improve the electrical conductivity of the electrolyte bath.

q: Which non-metal is used in the 'Lead Pencil'?
e: Pencil 'lead' is actually a mixture of graphite and clay; it contains no actual lead element.

q: What is 'Cupellation' used for?
o: Refining Silver/Gold to remove base metals like Lead
e: Impure metal is heated in a shallow cup (cupel) where impurities oxidize and are absorbed.

q: Which metal is used as 'Radiation Shielding' in nuclear power plants?
e: Lead's high density and high atomic number make it the best barrier against Gamma and X-rays.

q: Which element is found in 'Mithila paintings' (Madhubani art) as a traditional black pigment?
e: Traditional Madhubani artists use soot mixed with cow dung for black outlines.

q: Which silver salt is known as 'Lunar Caustic' and is used as an indelible ink during elections in India?
e: Silver Nitrate reacts with the proteins in the skin to form a black, light-stable stain that cannot be easily washed off.

q: What is 'Calomel' chemically, and what is its primary use in old medicine?
e: Calomel ($Hg_2Cl_2$) was historically used as a laxative and to treat syphilis, though it is toxic in high doses.

q: Which silver compound is used for 'Artificial Rain' (Cloud Seeding)?
e: The crystal structure of Silver Iodide is very similar to that of ice, allowing it to act as a nucleus for water droplets.

q: The 'Parkes Process' is used to remove which precious metal from Lead?
e: It relies on the fact that silver is 300 times more soluble in molten zinc than in molten lead.

q: What is 'Corrosive Sublimate'?
e: Unlike Calomel, Mercuric Chloride is highly soluble in water and extremely poisonous.

q: Which process is used to extract 'Gold' by dissolving it in a solution of Sodium Cyanide?
e: This is a leaching process where gold forms a soluble complex: $[Au(CN)_2]^-$.

q: What is 'Horn Silver'?
e: Chlorargyrite or Horn Silver is a native ore of silver that looks like wax or horn.

q: Which metal is used in 'Amalgams' for dental fillings?
e: Dental amalgam is an alloy of Mercury with Silver, Tin, and Copper.

q: What happens to 'Silver' jewelry when it turns black in the air (Tarnishing)?
o: It reacts with H2S to form Silver Sulfide (Ag2S)
e: Tarnishing is the reaction of silver with sulfur compounds in the atmosphere.

q: Which reagent is used in the laboratory to test for the presence of 'Aldehydes' by forming a Silver Mirror?
e: Tollen's reagent is an ammoniacal silver nitrate solution that reduces to metallic silver in the presence of aldehydes.

q: What is the 'Purity' of Sterling Silver?
e: Sterling silver contains 92.5% Silver and 7.5% other metals (usually Copper) for strength.

q: Which metal is used as a 'Liquid Mirror' in large telescopes due to its high reflectivity and fluidity?
e: Rotating liquid mercury forms a perfect parabolic shape, much cheaper than solid glass mirrors.

q: What is 'Vermilion' (Sindoor) chemically?
e: Cinnabar is the natural form; the purified synthetic form is known as Vermilion.

q: Which 'Halide' is used for taking the actual image in black and white photography?
e: Silver Bromide is highly sensitive to light and decomposes into silver particles that form the 'latent image'.

q: What is 'Fulminating Gold'?
o: An explosive compound made from Gold and Ammonia
e: It was the first high explosive known, discovered in the 16th century.

q: Which element is used to 'Fix' the image in photography by dissolving unreacted AgBr?
e: Hypo reacts with unreacted AgBr to form a soluble complex, preventing the photo from turning completely black.

q: What is the 'Density' of Mercury?
e: Mercury is 13.6 times denser than water, which is why it is used in barometers.

q: Which metal is 'Most Ductile', capable of being drawn into a wire miles long from just one gram?
e: Gold is the most malleable and most ductile of all metals.

q: What is 'Baryte' an ore of?
e: Baryte ($BaSO_4$) is the main source of Barium, used in drilling muds and 'Barium meals' for X-rays.

q: Which metal is the central atom in the 'Chlorophyll' molecule, making it essential for photosynthesis?
e: Magnesium (Mg) is to plants what Iron is to human blood; it captures light energy.

q: What is the primary mineral component of 'Human Bones' and teeth?
e: Bones are made of Hydroxyapatite, a crystalline form of Calcium Phosphate.

q: Which compound is known as 'Quicklime'?
e: Calcium Oxide (CaO) is used in making cement and glass.

q: What happens when 'Water' is added to Quicklime (CaO)?
o: It absorbs heat and cools down
e: This process is called 'Slaking of Lime', forming Calcium Hydroxide.

q: Which metal is used in 'Flashbulbs' of old cameras because it burns with a dazzling white light?
e: Magnesium ribbon burns intensely to produce MgO and high-intensity light.

q: What is 'Milk of Magnesia' used for in medicine?
e: Magnesium Hydroxide [Mg(OH)2] neutralizes excess stomach acid.

q: Which gas is evolved when Magnesium reacts with 'Boiling Water'?
e: Mg + H2O (hot) → MgO + H2. It does not react with cold water.

q: What is the chemical name of 'Gypsum'?
e: CaSO4 · 2H2O. When heated to 100°C, it turns into Plaster of Paris.

q: Which of the following is the 'Softest' metal in the Reactivity Series?
e: Sodium and Potassium are so soft they can be cut with a kitchen knife.

q: Which metal can displace 'Copper' from a Copper Sulfate solution?
e: Iron is more reactive than Copper; it displaces Cu to form FeSO4 (Green solution).

q: What is the 'Nature' of Calcium Oxide (CaO)?
e: Metal oxides like CaO are generally basic and form hydroxides with water.

q: Which 'Calcium' salt is used for removing moisture from gases (Desiccant)?
e: Calcium Chloride is highly hygroscopic and absorbs water vapor efficiently.

q: The 'Lustre' of metals is due to:
o: Reflection of light by free electrons
e: The mobile 'sea of electrons' on the surface reflects incident light.

q: Which metal is found in the 'Earth's Crust' in the highest percentage among all metals?
e: Aluminum is the most abundant metal (approx 8%), followed by Iron.

q: What is 'Epsom Salt' used for in Bihar’s traditional farming and medicine?
o: As a fertilizer and for muscle pain
e: Magnesium Sulfate [MgSO4 · 7H2O] is Epsom salt.

q: Which metal 'Floats' on water because the Hydrogen gas bubbles stick to its surface?
e: Calcium reacts less violently than Na, allowing bubbles to lift the metal to the surface.

q: What is 'Slaked Lime' chemically?
e: Calcium Hydroxide is used for whitewashing walls.

q: Which metal is used in 'Thermite Welding' to join railway tracks?
e: Aluminum powder reduces Iron Oxide in a highly exothermic reaction.

q: What is 'Dolomite' an ore of?
e: Dolomite is CaCO3 · MgCO3.

q: Which of the following is a 'Liquid' metal at 30°C (slightly above room temperature)?
e: Gallium melts at 29.7°C (it can melt in your palm).

q: What is the 'Hardness' of an element compared to on the Mohs scale? Which is 10?
e: Diamond is the hardest natural substance (Mohs 10).

q: Which metal is used in 'Lead' pencils?
e: Pencils contain Graphite (a non-metal), not Lead.

q: What is 'Mortar' in construction made of?
e: Traditional mortar uses Calcium Hydroxide to bind sand.

q: Which metal provides 'Strength' to the exoskeleton of sea shells?
e: Shells are primarily made of Calcium Carbonate.

q: What is the 'Reactivity' of Gold?
e: Gold stays shiny because it doesn't react with oxygen or moisture.

q: Which non-metal is the 'Hardest'?
e: Diamond is a non-metal allotrope of Carbon.

q: Which metal is used in 'Anodizing' to create a thick oxide layer?
e: Anodized Aluminum is corrosion-resistant and used in cookware.

q: What is the 'Charge' on an Alkaline Earth Metal ion?
e: Group 2 elements (Mg, Ca, etc.) lose 2 valence electrons.

q: Which metal is used in 'Galvanization'?
e: Zinc coating prevents Iron from rusting.

q: What is 'Calamine' lotion used for in skin treatments?
e: Calamine is Zinc Carbonate, often mixed with ferric oxide.

q: Which metal reacts with 'Steam' to form an Oxide and Hydrogen?
e: Iron + Steam → Fe3O4 + H2.

q: Which metal is the 'Best' conductor of heat?
e: Silver is the best, followed by Copper.

q: What is 'Plaster of Paris' used for?
e: POP hardens when mixed with water.

q: Which metal is kept under 'Kerosene'?
e: Potassium reacts violently with air and water.

q: What is 'Lime Water'?
e: It turns milky when CO2 is passed through it.

q: Which metal is used in 'Surgical' instruments because it doesn't rust?
e: Stainless Steel is an alloy of Iron, Chromium, and Nickel.

q: What is 'Malleability'?
o: Ability to be drawn into wires
Ability to be beaten into sheets
e: Gold is the most malleable metal.

q: Which metal has the 'Lowest' melting point?
e: Mercury is liquid at room temperature.

q: Which element is found in 'Enamel' of teeth?
e: Fluorine strengthens enamel to prevent decay.

q: What is 'Magnesite'?
e: An ore of Magnesium.

q: Which metal is 'Paramagnetic'?
e: Aluminum is weakly attracted to magnets.

q: What is 'Dead Burnt Plaster'?
e: Formed when Gypsum is heated above 200°C.

q: Which metal is 'Ductile' but a poor conductor compared to silver?
e: Lead is a relatively poor conductor of electricity.

q: What is the color of 'Magnesium Oxide'?
e: MgO is a white powdery solid.

q: Which metal displacement is used in the 'Thermite' process?
e: Aluminum reduces iron oxide to molten iron.

q: What is 'Quicklime' used to remove from soil?
e: Lime neutralizes acidic soil in Bihar's agriculture.

q: Which non-metal is 'Lustrous'?
e: Iodine crystals have a metallic shine.

q: What is 'Limestone' chemically?
e: Calcium Carbonate is found in marbles and limestone rocks.

q: Which metal has the 'Highest' density?
e: Osmium is the densest element.

q: What is the 'Nature' of Non-metal oxides?
e: Most non-metal oxides like SO2 and CO2 form acids in water.

q: Which metal is used in the 'Photoelectric Cell' because it emits electrons most easily when exposed to light?
e: Cesium has the lowest ionization energy, making it highly sensitive to light.

q: What is the 'Main Constituent' of the alloy 'Invar'?
e: Invar is used for making clocks and measuring tapes because it doesn't expand with heat.

q: Which process is used to separate 'Mercury' from its ore Cinnabar?
e: Heating HgS in air (roasting) directly yields Mercury vapor, which is then condensed.

q: What is 'White Gold' usually an alloy of?
e: White gold is made by mixing yellow gold with white metals and plating it with Rhodium.

q: Which metal is 'Sacrificed' to protect iron ships from rusting (Cathodic Protection)?
e: Blocks of more reactive metals are attached to the hull; they corrode instead of the iron.

q: What is 'Glass' chemically classified as?
e: Glass does not have a sharp melting point and flows very slowly over decades.

q: Which substance is added to 'Cement' to slow down its setting time?
e: Gypsum [CaSO4 · 2H2O] prevents the cement from hardening too quickly while being worked.

q: What is the primary raw material for the manufacture of 'Soap'?
e: Soap is made by the hydrolysis of fats with a strong base (Saponification).

q: Which gas is used in 'Neon' signs to produce a brilliant 'Red' light?
e: Pure neon gas in a discharge tube glows with a distinct red-orange hue.

q: What is 'Saponification'?
e: It is the reaction between an ester (fat) and an alkali (NaOH/KOH).

q: Which metal is used in 'Nuclear Reactors' as a coolant because of its high heat capacity?
e: Liquid sodium is an excellent thermal conductor used in fast breeder reactors.

q: What is 'Water Glass' chemically?
e: Sodium Silicate [$Na_2SiO_3$] is soluble in water and used as a specialized adhesive.

q: Which compound is used in 'Dry Cleaning' clothes?
e: Non-polar solvents are used to dissolve grease without damaging delicate fabrics.

q: What is the 'Hardness' of water caused by?
e: Sulfates and Bicarbonates of Ca and Mg prevent soap from lathering.

q: Which type of 'Glass' is used for making laboratory equipment like beakers?
e: Borosilicate glass can withstand high temperatures without cracking.

q: What is 'Portland Cement' mainly composed of?
e: It is a mixture of Calcium silicates and aluminates.

q: Which gas is evolved during the 'Saponification' of fats?
e: Saponification produces soap and glycerol; it is not a gas-releasing reaction.

q: What is 'Soft Soap' made using?
e: Potassium soaps (KOH) are softer and used in liquid soaps and shaving creams.

q: Which chemical is used as a 'Fire Extinguisher' in the form of a dry powder?
e: Baking soda releases CO2 when heated, which smothers the fire.

q: What is 'Safety Glass' (Laminated glass) made of?
o: Glass with a layer of plastic (PVB) in between
e: The plastic layer holds the glass pieces together if it breaks, preventing injury.

q: Which metal is the 'Main Constituent' of Bronze?
e: Bronze is an alloy of Copper and Tin.

q: What is 'Annealing' of glass?
e: Slow cooling removes internal stresses so the glass doesn't shatter easily.

q: Which substance is used as a 'Mordant' in dyeing clothes in Bihar’s textile industries?
e: Alum helps the dye stick to the fabric permanently.

q: What is 'Flint Glass' used for?
e: It has a high refractive index, perfect for telescopes and cameras.

q: Which chemical is known as the 'King of Chemicals'?
e: Sulfuric acid [$H_2SO_4$] is used in almost every industrial process.

q: What is 'Dynamite' primarily composed of?
e: Alfred Nobel invented it by stabilizing liquid nitroglycerin with silica.

q: Which element is used in the 'Vulcanization' of rubber?
e: Sulfur makes rubber harder and more resistant to temperature changes.

q: What is 'Bleaching Powder' chemically?
e: $CaOCl_2$ is used for disinfecting water and bleaching textiles.

q: Which type of 'Fertilizer' provides Nitrogen to plants?
e: Urea [$NH_2CONH_2$] is the most common nitrogenous fertilizer.

q: What is 'Bakelite'?
e: Used for making electrical switches because it is an insulator and doesn't melt.

q: Which metal is used in the 'Filament' of a heater?
e: Nichrome (Nickel + Chromium) has high resistance and doesn't oxidize at high heat.

q: What is 'Talcum Powder' made of?
e: Talc is the softest mineral, a hydrated magnesium silicate.

q: Which substance is used to produce 'Artificial Smoke' in movies?
e: Both dry ice in water and liquid nitrogen create thick white fog.

q: What is 'Teflon' used for?
e: Polytetrafluoroethylene (PTFE) is extremely slippery and heat-resistant.

q: Which chemical is used to 'Preserve' biological specimens in labs?
e: Formalin prevents the decay of tissues.

q: What is 'Rayon'?
e: It is made from wood pulp but processed chemically.

q: Which chemical is used to test for 'Starch' in food?
e: Iodine turns blue-black in the presence of starch.

q: What is 'Matchbox' striking surface made of?
e: Red phosphorus is safer and sparks when rubbed against glass powder.

q: Which gas is used for 'Welding' metals along with Oxygen?
e: Oxy-acetylene flame reaches temperatures over 3000°C.

q: What is 'Aspirin' chemically?
e: Used as a painkiller and to prevent heart attacks.

q: Which substance is added to 'LPG' to detect gas leaks by smell?
e: LPG is odorless; Ethyl Mercaptan has a strong 'rotten cabbage' smell.

q: What is 'Vinegar' chemically?
e: Used in food preservation and pickling.

q: Which metal is used in 'Rechargeable Batteries' of mobile phones?
e: Lithium-ion batteries are light and hold a lot of energy.

q: What is 'Nylon' primarily used for?
e: It is a very strong and elastic synthetic polymer.

q: Which acid is present in an 'Ant’s Sting'?
e: This acid causes the burning sensation when an ant bites.

q: What is 'Heavy Water' used for?
e: Deuterium Oxide [$D_2O$] slows down neutrons in nuclear fission.

q: Which metal is found in 'Liquid' state at room temperature?
e: Mercury [Hg] is the only metal that is liquid at standard conditions.

q: What is 'Plaster of Paris' chemically?
e: It is Calcium Sulfate Hemihydrate.

q: Which chemical is used as a 'Coagulant' in the purification of drinking water to settle suspended impurities?
e: Alum neutralizes the charge on clay particles, causing them to clump together and settle down.

q: What is 'White Vitriol' used for in the industrial manufacture of textiles and Rayon?
e: Zinc Sulfate Heptahydrate is known as White Vitriol and is used in the Rayon spinning bath.

q: Which substance is known as 'Artificial Silk'?
e: Rayon is made from regenerated cellulose and has a shine and texture similar to natural silk.

q: What is the 'Flash Point' of a fuel?
o: The temperature at which it freezes
The lowest temperature at which it gives off ignitable vapors
e: Flash point is a key safety metric in the petroleum and paint industries.

q: Which acid is used in the manufacture of 'Lead Storage Batteries' used in Bihar’s solar power setups?
e: 38% Sulfuric Acid ($H_2SO_4$) acts as the electrolyte in lead-acid batteries.

q: What is 'Superphosphate of Lime' used as?
e: It is made by reacting rock phosphate with sulfuric acid to make phosphorus more available to crops.

q: Which chemical is used to 'Remove Rust' stains from clothes?
e: Oxalic acid reacts with insoluble iron compounds in rust to form soluble complexes.

q: What is 'Glass Wool' used for in industries?
e: Glass wool is a fibrous material made from molten glass that traps air to prevent heat transfer.

q: Which gas is known as 'Laughing Gas' and used as a mild anesthetic in dentistry?
e: Nitrous oxide has euphoric effects and helps reduce pain during minor surgeries.

q: What is 'Solder' alloy primarily made of?
e: Solder has a low melting point, perfect for joining electrical components without melting them.
